hero.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $45,000 by December 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The 10-point drop to 51.5% suggests real money is rotating out of the "Bitcoin stays above $45k" trade, probably nervous about macro headwinds and a potential 2026 recession cycle that could force liquidations. At even odds, you're getting paid fairly for tail risk, but the market's underpricing how much institutional buying support has dried up since Q4 2024.

daily_take.headline: Bitcoin's 45K Floor Looks Too Cheap at 51% Odds Right Now
daily_take.deck: Bitcoin dipped 10 points overnight to 51.5% for a sub-45K close by end-2026, suggesting the market got spooked by something. Here's the thing: we're 23 months out on a crypto asset that moves 20% in a week. The price is treating this like a coin flip when conviction should flow from macro regime. A 51% probability on a 2-year horizon with $1.5M in volume? Sharp money should be sizing into the "no dip" side while retail hedges their fear.
